reasoning: Based on my analysis of USDA recall processes, I estimate a 28% probability that this recall will be closed by July 1, 2025. This is a Class II undeclared allergen recall (sesame) involving 64,008 pounds of pork bun products distributed to retail locations in New York. The recall was issued April 24, 2025, leaving only 67 days until July 1. Key factors: 1) USDA requires complete accounting of all affected product before closure (search_2_summary), 2) Products were shipped to retail locations across New York, making recovery complex and time-consuming, 3) Despite being Class II (lower risk), the 67-day window is relatively short for complete product accounting and regulatory oversight processes, 4) No updates or completion notices have been reported in available sources by May 5, 2025, suggesting the process is ongoing. The base rate for undeclared allergen recalls being closed within this timeframe appears low given the distribution complexity and regulatory requirements for full product accounting.
